digest.top_line: SF-SABR (N=1687): LC 90-93% at 2yr, G3+ 2.9%; single-fraction lung SABR feasible at scale across institutions.
digest.tldr: Thoracic and prostate carried the sharpest signals. SF-SABR pooled data (N=1687) backs single-fraction lung delivery with durable LC and low toxicity. At AUA 2026, PSMA PET staging challenges ePLND rationale: 47.7% of LN mets fall outside template, no Level 1 BCR benefit established.

sites.intro: PSMA-era staging reopens the PLND omission debate: imaging NPV ~96% plus absent Level 1 evidence recasts ePLND as morbidity without proven benefit in most pts.

studies.name: PSMA PET/CT for primary staging and ePLND utility in prostate cancer (AUA 2026 session)
studies.tldr: PSMA PET/CT NPV ~96% at primary staging; 47.7% of LN mets outside ePLND template; RCTs show no consistent BCR benefit from ePLND.
details: 📊 PSMA PET/CT NPV ~96% for LNI at primary staging (Yaxley et al, BJUI 2019; N=1253 consecutive men)
📊 47.7% of LN metastases outside ePLND template boundaries — missed regardless of dissection extent
⚠️ RCTs have not shown consistent BCR improvements from ePLND; session discussants acknowledge absent Level 1 evidence for oncologic benefit
🔍 AUA 2026 session: synthesizes retrospective cohort (N=1253), systematic lymphedema review, and perspective piece; no new RCT data presented

subdetails: Intermediate risk, PSMA PET negative: PLND may safely be omitted (missed LNs small, likely missed by ePLND too)
GG3: nomograms add value for patient selection
High-risk: individualized decision; prior PLND raises lymphedema risk if salvage pelvic XRT needed
If PLND performed: extended template required

details.text: ⚠️ PLND morbidity by treatment context (Clinckaert et al, Cancers 2022)
subdetails: Lower limb lymphedema: 19-29% after PLND+salvage pelvic XRT; genital lymphedema 2-22%
0-9% lower limb lymphedema after pelvic lymph node XRT alone

details: ⚠️ DVT/PE risk 6-10x higher with PLND vs no PLND (Tyritzis et al J Urol 2015, N=3544)
🔗 Alternative pathway: PSMA PET staging → post-op pelvic XRT may replace ePLND staging and limit lymphedema morbidity (Roberts et al, PCAN 2024)
studies.key_figure_caption: Primary staging Ga-PSMA PET/CT, N=1253 consecutive men; 47.7% of LN metastases outside ePLND template boundaries.

verdict.rationale: Session synthesizes retrospective cohort + reviews; no new RCT data; discussants acknowledge absent Level 1 evidence for ePLND oncologic benefit.
verdict.audience: Primary localized prostate cancer, all risk groups, evaluating RP + PLND staging

open_questions: Does targeted or sentinel PLND guided by PSMA PET improve outcomes vs omission?
Can PSMA PET-directed post-op pelvic RT replace ePLND with equivalent oncologic results?
Optimal nomogram cutoff for PLND in high-risk disease after negative PSMA PET?

sites.intro: Multicenter TMT benchmark: regimen quality (5-FU CRT, IGRT) predicts bladder preservation independently of pt factors.

studies.name: Bladder-preserving TMT for MIBC: multicenter prognostic factor analysis (ESTRO 2026)
studies.tldr: MIBC TMT: CLR 63.7%, salvage cystectomy 9.7% in 369-pt multicenter cohort; 5-FU CRT and IGRT quality independently predicted CLR.
details: 🔍 Multicenter retrospective cohort, Spain, 2010-2022; N=369, cT2-T4aN0M0, median age 76, 85.1% male
🔍 Primary EP: CLR; secondary: OS, CSS, recurrence patterns, salvage cystectomy
🔍 Multivariable logistic regression to identify CLR predictors across 24 centers
⚠️ Retrospective design; no randomized comparator vs radical cystectomy
⚠️ 24-center heterogeneity and 12-year span invite era effects and regimen mix confounding
🔗 CLR 63.7% and salvage cystectomy 9.7% align with BC2001 and RTOG 0712 TMT benchmark data

verdict.rationale: Retrospective multicenter series; no RC comparator. CLR rates and quality-factor findings consistent with established TMT landmark evidence.

open_questions: IGRT frequency threshold for optimal local control
Long-term OS/CSS vs matched radical cystectomy cohorts
5-FU vs cisplatin-based CRT: prospective comparison needed

sites.intro: Single-fraction SABR at scale: largest pooled lung dataset to date supports LC and toxicity profile across primary and oligometastatic settings.

studies.name: Single-fraction SABR for primary NSCLC and lung oligometastases (pooled analysis)
studies.tldr: SF-SABR in 1687 pts: LC 90-93% at 2yr, G3+ AEs 2.9%, mOS 40mo (NSCLC) / 51mo (oligomets).
details: 🔍 Pooled retrospective, 3 institutions (Peter Mac / Cleveland Clinic / Roswell Park): 1200 primary NSCLC + 487 pulmonary oligomets
📊 LC 90-93% at 2yr; isolated local/locoregional failure uncommon in both cohorts

details.text: ⚠️ AEs reported for primary NSCLC only (n=789; no Roswell Park data)
subdetails: Most common: pleural effusion 14%, pneumonitis 7%, dyspnea 4%

details: ⚠️ No randomized control arm; no prospective comparison to multi-fraction SABR regimens
🔗 Multi-fraction SABR (3-5fx) is established SOC for early NSCLC; single-fraction delivery at this scale is novel, consistent LC supports feasibility

verdict.rationale: Single-arm retrospective pooled series; no randomized comparison to standard multi-fraction SABR for primary NSCLC or oligomets.

open_questions: Randomized comparison of single vs multi-fraction SABR for primary NSCLC needed
Optimal tumor selection for single fraction: size, location, centrality?
OS durability beyond current follow-up, particularly in oligomets?

Purpose
Trimodality therapy (TMT), combining maximal transurethral resection of the bladder tumor followed by concurrent chemoradiotherapy, has emerged as a bladder-preserving alternative to radical
cystectomy (RC) for muscle-invasive bladder cancer (MIBC). Despite growing evidence, real-world multicenter data from European populations remain limited.

Conclusions
• For intermediate risk disease PLND may safely be omitted if PSMA PET is negative for LNI,
missed LN are small and may be missed in ePLND as well
• In GG3 disease nomograms can be of additional use
•
For high-risk disease, the decision is individual and should be discussed with the patient at
length including the possibility that an adjuvant/salvage pelvic RT may increase side effects
If PLND is to be performed, then it should be in extended fashion

PERSPECTIVE

1f) Chock for updates
Clinical
Is extended pelvic lymph node dissection REALLY required for
staging of prostate cancer in the PSMA-PET era?
Matthew J. Roberts 123, John W. Yaxley 134, Johan Stranne, Inge M. van Oort and Derya Tilki C89.10
Strengths
•Confirm metastases not detected by imaging (false-negatives)
• Accurate histopathological staging (false-positives)
• Total tumour burden reduction via excision (if nodal metastases
present)
Opportunities
•Incorporation with PSMA-PET imaging for targeted (Radioguided/
Sentinel) LND
• Improved patient selection considering prevalence of metastatic
disease (higher nomogram cut-off)
Weaknesses
• Limitation of anatomical template (missed metastases outside
template)
• Limitation of histopathology (may not detect all metastatic cells)
•Associated morbidity/complications, impact on health and quality of
life
• Increased intra-operative time
Threats
• Absent Level 1 evidence for significant oncological benefit
• Increasing demand on health services to limit low-value care/
interventions
• Surgeon proficiency/training
• Earlier use of androgen receptor signalling inhibitors and other
medications (pN status less relevant)
Alternative care pathways instead of routine eLND
PSMA-PET for staging may prompt a plan for post-operative pelvic lymph node radiotherapy,
limiting morbidity such as lymphedema, especially in the absence of a prior eND.
PCAN, 2024

• Pulmonary Oligometastases: Median PHS 11
